stories.headline: Danantara Starts Bond Sale in Test After Indonesian Selloff
stories.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time when confidence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy has soured.

stories.headline: Thai Long Bonds Draw Funds With Steepest Curve in Emerging Asia
stories.summary: Thailand’s yield curve has shifted to the steepest in emerging Asia, with some investors saying that makes its longer-dated bonds look attractive given a divergence in interest-rate expectations there versus regional peers.

stories.headline: India Turns to Military to Beef Up Security After Exam Scandals
stories.summary: India is taking unprecedented measures to restore confidence in its national student examination system after a series of scandals fueled angry protests from young people and put pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government.

stories.headline: Activist Elliott Hits Back at Gold Miner Northern Star Over Sale
stories.summary: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management LP has hit back at Australia’s biggest gold stock Northern Star Resources Ltd. by urging the beleaguered miner’s board to take urgent action and reconsider a sale as its valuation flounders.

stories.headline: New Zealand Primary Exports Seen Slower After Stellar 2026 Gain
stories.summary: New Zealand’s target of doubling primary exports by 2034 remains in place even as the stellar growth of recent years is tipped to slow in the face of geopolitical and weather challenges.

stories.headline: Maasai women turn drought into income through fodder farming in Tanzania
stories.summary: As drought kills livestock, Maasai women are turning drought-resistant grass into animal feed and income.

stories.headline: US Launches Second Round of Strikes on Iran

stories.headline: Key Military Mineral Stores Depleted By Iran War Says US Mining Heads
stories.summary: Paul Andre Huet, chairman & CEO of Americas Gold & Silver, and Gary Evans, CEO of US Antimony, discussed the impact of war with Iran on US critical minerals supplies and how the ramp up in tech IPOs has increased demand, even as metals prices have slumped in recent days. Huet, speaking on the rise of resources for artificial intelligence IPOs, said that silver is going to be the 'new power revolution.'  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Global Citizen CEO on First-Ever FIFA Halftime Show

Curated by Chris Martin of Coldplay, the halftime event will feature performances by Madonna, BTS, and Shakira, and is expected to be the most viewed halftime show in history. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.
